name: Polish Yeast Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk in a saucepan over low heat until it begins to boil gently.
2. Stir in the sugar, salt, and butter until the butter melts completely. Allow the mixture to cool to lukewarm temperature.
3. In a large bowl, sprinkle yeast over warm water (approximately 110°F/43°C) and stir until dissolved.
4. Add the milk mixture, eggs, and flour to the yeast mixture. Beat vigorously for about five minutes or until a smooth batter forms.
5. Cover the bowl with a clean towel and let the batter rise in a warm place, away from drafts, for 1 1/2 hours or until doubled in volume.
6. Gently fold in the chopped almonds, raisins, and lemon peel into the risen batter.
7. Grease and flour a 1 1/2-quart charlotte mold or deep cake pan. Pour the batter into the prepared mold.
8. Let the batter rise for an additional hour at room temperature.
9. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Bake the cake in the center of the oven for about 50 minutes, until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Remove from the oven and let cool in the mold for 20 minutes before turning it out onto a serving platter.
11. In a small bowl, whisk together confectioners' sugar and 1 tablespoon milk to form a glaze.
12. Drizzle the glaze over the cooled cake and garnish with whole candied cherries. Serve immediately.
